 {"id":"ft-fdwn","title":"FTUI-01.3 Create self-contained parity contract (ratatui behavior -> ftui behavior)","description":"## Background\nFuture contributors need a concrete contract that defines what must remain behaviorally identical and what can intentionally change.\n\n## Deliverables\n- parity matrix per screen/interaction\n- explicit intentional deltas with rationale\n- acceptance checklist used by E5/E6/E7 tasks\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- parity matrix covers all current TUI views and critical actions\n- matrix can be used standalone without legacy plan docs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:34.679717868Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.324786Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T00:50:47.673094709Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-fdwn","depends_on_id":"ft-euty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-fdwn","depends_on_id":"ft-jf8c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-ferw","title":"Tests: wait-for/quiescence helpers (unit+integration)","description":"# Task: Tests for wait-for/quiescence helpers

## Goal
Prevent regressions in the timing primitives.

## Requirements
- Unit tests for:
  - backoff schedule behavior
  - timeout error contents
- Integration tests for:
  - quiescence with synthetic producer/consumer

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail with actionable output when timing primitives regress.

 {"id":"ft-fjiz","title":"E2E: schema/docs/client pipeline with detailed logging","description":"## Goal
Validate schema-driven docs and client generation end-to-end with verbose logs and artifacts.

## Requirements
- Run schema→docs/types generation in CI mode with verbose logging.
- Execute a typed client against wa robot endpoints and validate schema conformance.
- Capture generated docs/types and logs as artifacts on failure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E pipeline passes locally and in CI.
- Failure artifacts include logs plus generated docs/types outputs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftBeacon","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:54.359763733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.303596Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T23:19:25.583849911Z","close_reason":"Created scripts/e2e_schema_pipeline.sh - validates robot commands against JSON schemas. Fixed schema drift (added pane_uuid to wa-robot-state.json). 63 tests pass, 2 skipped for unimplemented docs/types generation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-fjiz","depends_on_id":"ft-2vr1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-fjiz","depends_on_id":"ft-mxzp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-fjiz","depends_on_id":"ft-upg.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-fled9","title":"[audit][T1.2] Wire TuningConfig into ObservationRuntime and subsystems","description":"# Wire TuningConfig into ObservationRuntime and subsystems

## What
Make TuningConfig available to all subsystems that currently use hard-coded constants:

1. Load TuningConfig from ft.toml [tuning] section during FtConfig::load()
2. Store as Arc<TuningConfig> in ObservationRuntime
3. Pass to subsystems during initialization (PatternEngine, PolicyEngine, IngestPipeline, etc.)
4. Subsystems read from TuningConfig fields instead of module-level const values

## Key Integration Points
- runtime.rs: ObservationRuntime holds Arc<TuningConfig>
- config.rs: FtConfig gains a tuning: TuningConfig field
- Each subsystem constructor gains a tuning param or reads from runtime context

## Design Note
Do NOT make TuningConfig mutable at runtime yet. This is load-once-at-startup. Hot-reload of tuning values is a future enhancement (would require watch + atomic swap).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. FtConfig loads [tuning] section from ft.toml
2. ObservationRuntime exposes tuning() -> &TuningConfig
3. At least one subsystem (e.g., runtime.rs backpressure thresholds) reads from TuningConfig instead of const
4. Missing [tuning] section produces default values (backward compatible)
5. Invalid [tuning] values produce clear parse errors at startup","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:35.674922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:50:59.676527Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","config","infrastructure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-fled9","depends_on_id":"ft-t2keh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:45.870143Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-fled9","title":"[audit][T1.2] Wire TuningConfig into ObservationRuntime and subsystems","description":"# Wire TuningConfig into ObservationRuntime and subsystems

## What
Make TuningConfig available to all subsystems that currently use hard-coded constants:

1. Load TuningConfig from ft.toml [tuning] section during FtConfig::load()
2. Store as Arc<TuningConfig> in ObservationRuntime
3. Pass to subsystems during initialization (PatternEngine, PolicyEngine, IngestPipeline, etc.)
4. Subsystems read from TuningConfig fields instead of module-level const values

## Key Integration Points
- runtime.rs: ObservationRuntime holds Arc<TuningConfig>
- config.rs: FtConfig gains a tuning: TuningConfig field
- Each subsystem constructor gains a tuning param or reads from runtime context

## Design Note
Do NOT make TuningConfig mutable at runtime yet. This is load-once-at-startup. Hot-reload of tuning values is a future enhancement (would require watch + atomic swap).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. FtConfig loads [tuning] section from ft.toml
2. ObservationRuntime exposes tuning() -> &TuningConfig
3. At least one subsystem (e.g., runtime.rs backpressure thresholds) reads from TuningConfig instead of const
4. Missing [tuning] section produces default values (backward compatible)
5. Invalid [tuning] values produce clear parse errors at startup","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:35.674922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:58:20.638272Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:58:20.638025Z","close_reason":"TuningConfig wired into ObservationRuntime with with_tuning/tuning/tuning_arc. Also added tuning field to Config struct. Compiles clean.  {"id":"ft-fno","title":"[EPIC] Plugin/Extension System: custom patterns, workflows, and integrations","description":"# [EPIC] Plugin/Extension System

## Mission
Enable users to extend wa with custom patterns, workflows, and integrations without modifying core code.

## Why This Matters
Every user has unique needs:
- Custom agent patterns not in built-in packs
- Proprietary workflow logic
- Integration with internal tools
- Organization-specific safety rules

A plugin system enables:
- **Customization** without forking
- **Sharing** of community extensions
- **Isolation** of custom code
- **Iteration** without core upgrades

## Core Insight: Extensibility as a Feature
The most successful developer tools are extensible (VS Code, Neovim, etc.). wa should be too.

## Scope

### Pattern Extensions
- User-defined pattern packs
- Custom extraction rules
- Pattern pack inheritance (extend built-in)

### Workflow Extensions
- Custom workflow definitions (YAML/TOML)
- Custom step implementations (Lua/WASM)
- Workflow hooks (pre/post)

### Integration Extensions
- Notification channels (Slack, Discord, custom)
- Export formatters
- Data sources

### Extension Management
- `wa ext install` / `wa ext list` / `wa ext remove`
- Local extensions directory
- Optional registry (community sharing)

## Success Criteria
- Users can add custom patterns without code changes
- Custom workflows run safely (sandboxed)
- Extensions are easy to share
- No security vulnerabilities from extensions

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for extension loading
- Sandbox tests for untrusted code
- Integration tests for extension lifecycle
- Security tests for malicious extensions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pattern packs loadable from user directory
- [ ] Custom workflows definable in YAML/TOML
- [ ] Extension management CLI works
- [ ] Extensions sandboxed (WASM or Lua)
- [ ] Documentation covers extension authoring
- [ ] Tests cover all extension types","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:54:32.094417025Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-09T18:09:38.167825399Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T18:09:38.167760308Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-fno","depends_on_id":"ft-8bk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-fno.1","title":"User pattern packs: load custom rules from ~/.config/wa/patterns/","description":"# User pattern packs

## Purpose
Allow users to define custom pattern packs that load alongside built-in packs.

## User Pack Location
```
~/.config/wa/patterns/
├── my-org-patterns/
│   ├── pack.toml        # Pack metadata
│   └── rules.toml       # Rule definitions
└── custom-agent/
    ├── pack.toml
    └── rules.toml
```

## Pack Format
```toml
# pack.toml
[pack]
name = "my-org-patterns"
version = "1.0.0"
description = "Custom patterns for internal agents"
extends = "core.codex"  # Optional: inherit from built-in

# rules.toml
[[rules]]
id = "my-org.custom_warning"
name = "Custom Warning Pattern"
pattern = "\\[INTERNAL\\] Warning: (.+)"
severity = "warning"
extract = { message = "$1" }
agent_types = ["codex", "custom-agent"]
```

## Loading Priority
1. Built-in packs (highest priority)
2. User packs in order found
3. Workspace-local packs (.wa/patterns/)

For conflicts, later packs override earlier ones unless `extends` is used.

## Implementation
```rust
pub fn load_all_packs(config: &Config) -> Result<Vec<PatternPack>> {
    let mut packs = vec![];
    
    // Load built-in
    packs.extend(load_builtin_packs()?);
    
    // Load user packs
    let user_dir = config.patterns_dir();
    if user_dir.exists() {
        for entry in fs::read_dir(user_dir)? {
            let pack = PatternPack::load(entry?.path())?;
            packs.push(pack);
        }
    }
    
    // Load workspace packs
    if let Some(workspace_dir) = config.workspace_patterns_dir() {
        // Similar loading
    }
    
    // Resolve inheritance and conflicts
    resolve_pack_inheritance(&mut packs)?;
    
    Ok(packs)
}
```

## Validation
User packs are validated on load:
- Valid TOML syntax
- Required fields present
- Regex patterns compile
- No duplicate rule IDs (within pack)

## Testing
- Unit tests for pack loading
- Tests for inheritance resolution
- Tests for conflict handling
- Tests for invalid pack rejection

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User packs load from ~/.config/wa/patterns/
- [ ] Pack format documented
- [ ] Inheritance (extends) works
- [ ] Conflict resolution is predictable
- [ ] Invalid packs rejected with clear errors
- [ ] Tests cover all scenarios","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"NavyMeadow","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:54:46.618300818Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-09T18:08:35.808106091Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T18:08:35.808039287Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-fno.1","depends_on_id":"ft-fno","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-fno.1","depends_on_id":"ft-nu4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-fno.2","title":"Custom workflows: YAML/TOML definitions with step sequences","description":"# Custom workflows

## Purpose
Allow users to define custom workflows in YAML/TOML without writing Rust code.

## Workflow Definition Format
```yaml
# ~/.config/wa/workflows/my_custom_workflow.yaml
name: my_custom_workflow
description: "Handle our custom agent's restart prompt"
triggers:
  - event_type: "my-org.restart_prompt"
    agent_types: ["custom-agent"]

steps:
  - name: wait_for_stable
    action: wait
    condition:
      type: pane_idle
      timeout: 5s
  
  - name: send_restart_command
    action: send_text
    text: "/restart\n"
    policy: require_approval  # or allow, deny
  
  - name: verify_restart
    action: wait
    condition:
      type: pattern
      pattern: "Agent restarted successfully"
      timeout: 30s

on_failure:
  action: notify
  message: "Custom workflow failed at step: ${failed_step}"
```

## Step Actions
```yaml
# Available actions
actions:
  - send_text      # Send keystrokes to pane
  - wait           # Wait for condition
  - notify         # Send notification
  - log            # Write to audit log
  - abort          # Stop workflow
  - conditional    # If/else branching
  - loop           # Repeat steps
```

## Implementation
```rust
pub struct YamlWorkflow {
    name: String,
    description: String,
    triggers: Vec<WorkflowTrigger>,
    steps: Vec<WorkflowStep>,
    on_failure: Option<FailureHandler>,
}

impl Workflow for YamlWorkflow {
    async fn execute(&self, ctx: &mut WorkflowContext) -> Result<WorkflowResult> {
        for step in &self.steps {
            match self.execute_step(step, ctx).await {
                Ok(StepResult::Continue) => continue,
                Ok(StepResult::Abort) => break,
                Err(e) => {
                    if let Some(handler) = &self.on_failure {
                        handler.execute(ctx, &e).await?;
                    }
                    return Err(e);
                }
            }
        }
        Ok(WorkflowResult::Success)
    }
}
```

## Testing
- Unit tests for YAML parsing
- Step action tests
- Conditional and loop tests
- Failure handling tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] YAML format supports all required actions
- [ ] Workflows load from user directory
- [ ] Steps execute in sequence
- [ ] Conditions work correctly
- [ ] Failure handlers execute
- [ ] Documentation covers workflow authoring
- [ ] Tests cover all step types","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeFox","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:55:02.320177158Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:16:18.977157107Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T09:16:18.977080405Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-fno.2","depends_on_id":"ft-fno","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-fno.2","depends_on_id":"ft-nu4.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
